reasoning: The document contains a programmatic CRITICAL defect regarding header and footer elements retained across multiple pages (pages_affected=5/5), which creates pervasive accessibility issues for screen readers. It also contains a programmatic MAJOR defect for mixed numbering types indicating list flattening. Based on the count-based routing rules, any critical defect requires an automatic REJECT.

concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (2 list(s)): p1:letter+roman, p5:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=2. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The source document contains underlined and bolded text for the main document title and the lead-in words of the bullet points in Sections II and III. This emphasis is entirely lost in the output JSON, which contains no underline formatting or markdown bold asterisks.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Dam Safety – There was a concern about the history... (where "Dam Safety" is bolded and underlined)
concerns.output_quote: "Dam Safety – There was a concern about the history of structural failures in dams."

action_items: Remove retained header/footer artifacts from the rendered text
Fix list structures to prevent flattening of sub-points
Restore bold and underline emphasis for document titles and lead-in words
